verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP/GRP78) is canonically ER-luminal, but has documented cell-state-induced surface display in cancer and stress contexts. Tumor cells and stressed cells externalize HSPA5 to the plasma membrane outer leaflet, where it functions as an immunogenic ecto-chaperone and immunosuppressive factor. Cancer-directed immunotherapies and monoclonal antibodies target surface HSPA5 on tumor cells. This non-baseline ecto-pool qualifies as contextual via cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation and ER stress-related externalization).
